refined_fr.refined_fr: CR-NGHE-005 Refined: The NGHE solution shall provide an integrated performance requirement derivation, recording, and management system that translates worksite mission context (site conditions, operational policies, safety constraints) into documented performance targets (cycle time, energy efficiency, availability, safety margins), maintains a complete audit trail of baseline establishment and calibration updates, and ensures traceability from worksite inputs through performance targets to system design parameters.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is outcome-focused but lacks explicit mention of traceability, audit trail, and the bidirectional linkage between worksite context and performance targets. Refinement clarifies that this is a closed-loop system requirement with documented evidence of derivation and change management, critical for safety-critical systems and regulatory compliance.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Verification shall demonstrate: (1) a documented performance target set derived from a representative worksite mission context; (2) complete traceability matrix linking each worksite input to derived performance targets; (3) audit log showing baseline establishment timestamp, author, and all calibration/update events with justification; (4) acceptance by Site Manager and Safety Manager that targets reflect worksite constraints.
sub_frs: CR-NGHE-005.1: The system shall capture and structure worksite mission context inputs (site conditions, operational policies, safety plan constraints).
CR-NGHE-005.2: The system shall derive performance targets (cycle time, energy efficiency, availability, safety margins) from worksite mission context using documented derivation rules.
CR-NGHE-005.3: The system shall record and maintain a complete audit trail of performance target baseline establishment and all calibration/update events.
CR-NGHE-005.4: The system shall maintain bidirectional traceability from worksite inputs to performance targets to system design parameters.
CR-NGHE-005.5: The system shall provide access control and change management for performance target updates, with approval workflows for safety-critical changes.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Centralized performance management database with worksite context schema, derivation rule engine, and audit logging; integrated with telemetry and tasking interfaces for bidirectional traceability.
DP-B: Distributed performance target management across Vehicle and Teleoperation modules with periodic synchronization and a central audit log aggregator.
DP-C: Manual performance target derivation and recording via Site Manager interface with post-hoc audit trail reconstruction from system logs.
DP-D: Hybrid approach: centralized derivation rule engine and audit log, with performance target caching in Vehicle and Teleoperation modules for operational efficiency.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-D: Hybrid centralized-distributed architecture with a dedicated Performance Requirement Management Engine (PRME) that owns derivation rules, audit logging, and traceability; performance targets are cached in Vehicle and Teleoperation modules for operational use, with synchronization and validation on every mission context change. The PRME exposes Tasking (for target distribution) and Telemetry (for context capture and validation) interfaces.

potential_sources: Coupling between PRME derivation rules and Vehicle/Teleoperation operational logic is mitigated by treating targets as read-only configuration pushed via Tasking interface.
Coupling between audit trail and real-time telemetry is managed by asynchronous logging; telemetry is not blocked by audit writes.
Coupling between worksite context capture and Safety Services is managed via explicit SafetyAlert interface for safety-critical constraint violations.
coupling_check.notes: DP-D avoids tight coupling by separating derivation (centralized, infrequent) from operational use (distributed, frequent). Synchronization is event-driven on mission context changes, not continuous polling.

assumptions_risks: ASSUMPTION: Worksite mission context is provided by Site Manager or external system with sufficient structure and completeness.
ASSUMPTION: Derivation rules are stable and can be codified; rule changes are infrequent and managed via change control.
RISK: If derivation rules are complex or context-dependent, rule engine may become a bottleneck or source of errors; mitigation: rule validation and testing framework required.
RISK: Cache synchronization failures between PRME and Vehicle/Teleoperation could lead to stale targets; mitigation: version tracking and validation checksums on every use.
RISK: Audit log growth could impact storage and query performance; mitigation: archival and indexing strategy required.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Clarify the derivation rule specification language and validation framework. Define the frequency and trigger conditions for cache synchronization. Specify audit log retention and archival policy.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module owns the Performance Requirement Management Engine (PRME), which includes: (1) worksite mission context schema and capture interface; (2) performance target derivation rule engine; (3) audit trail database and logging service; (4) traceability matrix maintenance; (5) change management and approval workflows for safety-critical targets. The PRME is the single source of truth for performance targets and audit history. Vehicle and Teleoperation modules consume performance targets via Tasking interface and report context/telemetry via Telemetry interface.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution receives worksite mission context, operational state, and performance measurement data from Vehicle and Teleoperation modules to validate and update performance targets.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution distributes derived performance targets and operational constraints to Vehicle and Teleoperation modules for execution.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution receives safety constraint inputs from Safety Services and sends performance target updates that affect safety margins; bidirectional for safety-critical change notifications.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution uses Network Link for communication with remote Site Manager and Tele-operator interfaces for performance target review, approval, and audit trail access.

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the natural owner of system-level performance requirement management. The PRME is a core system service that must be centralized for traceability and audit integrity. Minimal diffusion: only performance target caching in Vehicle/Teleoperation is distributed, but derivation and audit remain centralized.

Vehicle module could cache performance targets for operational use, but should NOT own derivation, audit, or traceability logic. Assigning PRME to Vehicle would couple performance management to vehicle-specific logic and fragment audit trail across multiple vehicle instances.
Teleoperation module is a consumer of performance targets, not an owner. Assigning PRME to Teleoperation would create asymmetry with Vehicle and complicate multi-vehicle coordination.

Safety Services could own safety-specific performance targets (e.g., safety margins, emergency response times), but general performance requirement management (cycle time, energy efficiency, availability) is outside Safety Services scope. Hybrid ownership would fragment the PRME and complicate traceability.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: NGHE Solution is the optimal owner because: (1) it is the system-level integrator responsible for end-to-end mission performance; (2) performance requirement derivation is a system-level concern that must be independent of any single vehicle or teleoperation instance; (3) audit trail and traceability must be centralized for regulatory compliance and safety assurance; (4) the PRME is a shared service consumed by multiple downstream modules (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services).
containment_risks: RISK: If NGHE Solution becomes a monolithic module, the PRME could be difficult to test and maintain independently; mitigation: design PRME as a cohesive but modular subsystem with clear internal boundaries.
RISK: If worksite context capture is not well-structured, the PRME could receive incomplete or inconsistent inputs; mitigation: define a strict worksite context schema and validation rules.
RISK: If Vehicle or Teleoperation modules cache stale performance targets, they could operate outside intended parameters; mitigation: implement version tracking and periodic validation against PRME.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Define the internal architecture of PRME (e.g., separate derivation engine, audit service, traceability service). Specify the worksite context schema and validation rules. Define cache synchronization protocol and version management between NGHE Solution and Vehicle/Teleoperation modules.

fr_dp_gate.reason: DP-D (hybrid centralized-distributed PRME) directly addresses all aspects of the refined FR: (1) captures and structures worksite mission context; (2) derives performance targets using a rule engine; (3) maintains audit trail with centralized logging; (4) enables traceability via PRME ownership; (5) supports change management via Tasking interface. The DP is neither over-constrained nor under-specified; it provides a clear solution concept without prescribing implementation details.

diffusion_risks: Performance target caching in Vehicle and Teleoperation modules introduces a minor diffusion of target data, but this is justified for operational efficiency and is mitigated by centralized derivation and audit.
Worksite context capture may involve multiple sources (Site Manager, sensors, external systems); this is managed by a well-defined context schema and validation rules in PRME.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: NGHE Solution is the clear owner of the PRME. The module boundary is well-defined: PRME owns derivation, audit, and traceability; Vehicle and Teleoperation own only target caching and operational use. External interfaces (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, Network Link) are all known and appropriate for this module.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible via: (1) functional testing of derivation rules against representative worksite contexts; (2) audit trail inspection for completeness and correctness; (3) traceability matrix review; (4) integration testing of Tasking and Telemetry interfaces; (5) acceptance testing with Site Manager and Safety Manager. All verification activities are within the scope of NGHE Solution module testing.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The allocation of CR-NGHE-005 to NGHE Solution is clear and unambiguous. Sub-FRs are allocated to specific PRME subsystems (context capture, derivation engine, audit service, traceability service, change management). No allocation conflicts or gaps are identified.

recommended_revisions: Refine the worksite mission context schema to include all required inputs (site conditions, operational policies, safety constraints, performance measurement data).
Define the derivation rule specification language and provide examples for cycle time, energy efficiency, and availability targets.
Specify the audit log schema, retention policy, and query interface for traceability and compliance reporting.
Define the change management workflow for performance target updates, including approval roles and safety-critical change triggers.

decision.justification: CR-NGHE-005 is not atomic and requires further decomposition. The refined FR identifies five sub-FRs (context capture, derivation, audit trail, traceability, change management) that are distinct functional concerns and should be decomposed to the next level. The selected DP (hybrid PRME) is sound and passes all gates, but the IM assignment (NGHE Solution) is at a high level of abstraction. Decomposition will clarify the internal architecture of PRME and allocate sub-FRs to specific PRME subsystems or components.
next_level_sub_frs: CR-NGHE-005.1: The system shall capture and structure worksite mission context inputs (site conditions, operational policies, safety plan constraints).
CR-NGHE-005.2: The system shall derive performance targets (cycle time, energy efficiency, availability, safety margins) from worksite mission context using documented derivation rules.
CR-NGHE-005.3: The system shall record and maintain a complete audit trail of performance target baseline establishment and all calibration/update events.
CR-NGHE-005.4: The system shall maintain bidirectional traceability from worksite inputs to performance targets to system design parameters.
CR-NGHE-005.5: The system shall provide access control and change management for performance target updates, with approval workflows for safety-critical changes.

assumptions: Worksite mission context is provided by Site Manager or external system with sufficient structure and completeness.
Derivation rules are stable and can be codified; rule changes are infrequent and managed via change control.
Performance targets are read-only from the perspective of Vehicle and Teleoperation modules; updates are pushed via Tasking interface.
Audit trail is immutable and tamper-evident; historical records are never deleted or modified.
Safety-critical performance targets (e.g., safety margins) are coordinated with Safety Services via SafetyAlert interface.
open_risks: Derivation rule complexity and maintainability; rule engine may become a bottleneck or source of errors.
Cache synchronization failures between PRME and Vehicle/Teleoperation could lead to stale targets.
Audit log growth could impact storage and query performance.
Worksite context capture may be incomplete or inconsistent if not well-structured.
Change management workflow may be too rigid or too permissive for safety-critical targets.
evidence_needed: Worksite mission context schema and examples from representative worksites.
Derivation rule specification language and validation framework.
Audit log schema, retention policy, and query interface design.
Change management workflow and approval role definitions.
Cache synchronization protocol and version management design.
Performance targets for representative mission scenarios (cycle time, energy efficiency, availability, safety margins).
